title: Moving & Storage Company in Burgess Hill: Local Guide
meta: Removals and storage in Burgess Hill, West Sussex. A local guide to choosing a moving and storage company, with access tips and costs.
h1: Choosing a Moving and Storage Company in Burgess Hill
lead: Burgess Hill is one of the most practical and well-connected places to live in mid Sussex, which makes it a popular spot to move to and around. This local guide covers how to choose a moving and storage company in Burgess Hill, what to ask, and how to plan a move that runs smoothly from start to finish.
body:
Sitting in mid Sussex between Haywards Heath and Brighton, Burgess Hill has grown over the years into a busy, family-friendly town with excellent transport links, plenty of green space and a steady supply of housing across all budgets. It is the kind of place people move to for the practical reasons: a fast train to London and the coast, good schools, and more house for your money than some of its pricier neighbours. As a family-run removals and storage firm that has served mid Sussex since 2016, we handle moves into and around Burgess Hill regularly, so this guide blends genuine local knowledge with practical advice on choosing the right company and planning your move well.

What makes Burgess Hill a good place to move to

Burgess Hill's strongest card is its location. The railway station runs frequent services north to London and Gatwick and south to Brighton and the coast, making it a genuine commuter base while keeping the sea and the South Downs within easy reach. The A23 is close by for road travel, linking the town quickly to the motorway network and the airport.

Beyond the connections, the town offers a lot of practical appeal. There is generous green space, including the much-loved St John's Park and the network of green corridors that thread through the town, ongoing investment in the town centre, a good spread of schools, and a range of housing from period homes to large modern developments on the edges of town. That mix of affordability, connectivity and amenity is exactly why so many families and first-time buyers choose Burgess Hill, and why it sits comfortably alongside neighbours like Haywards Heath and Hassocks as a mid-Sussex favourite.

Why local knowledge matters for a Burgess Hill move

A removals firm that knows Burgess Hill plans for the things that catch others out. The town has a wide mix of property types, each with its own moving-day quirks. The newer estates on the edges of town are spacious and modern, but the lorry often cannot park right at the door, so a crew that knows the layout plans the carry accordingly. Older homes closer to the centre can have tighter access, narrower staircases and on-street parking that needs thinking about in advance.

Knowing the local road network matters too, from the busier stretches that are best avoided at school run and rush hour to the quieter routes that keep a move on schedule. Our dedicated Burgess Hill removals service is built around this kind of local familiarity, so the day runs to plan rather than turning into a series of improvised problems.

What to look for in a moving and storage company

Whether you need removals, storage or both, a few essential checks separate a reliable firm from a risky one. The cheapest quote is rarely the best value, and a little due diligence protects your belongings and your peace of mind.

Proper insurance

Always ask whether a firm carries goods-in-transit and public liability cover, and for how much. We are fully insured with cover up to £10 million, so your possessions are protected from the moment they are loaded, through transit and storage, to the moment they reach their destination. This matters just as much for items in storage as for those in the van.

Accreditation and genuine reviews

Independent verification is worth far more than self-written reviews. We are a member of LAPADA, the association for art and antiques dealers, and verified by Checkatrade, both of which give you an objective measure of how we work. Reading genuine customer reviews on independent platforms is one of the best ways to judge any local firm before you commit.

Secure, flexible storage

If storage is part of your plan, ask how the facility is secured, whether your belongings are stored cleanly and dry, and how easily you can access them. Our storage solutions are designed to be both secure and flexible, whether you need a few days or several months. You can estimate how much space you will need using our storage calculator before you commit to anything.

A clear, itemised quote

A trustworthy firm sets out exactly what is included rather than offering a single vague figure. Be cautious of quotes that look too good to be true, as they often leave out packing materials, furniture dismantling or storage handling fees. Our pricing page explains how we structure costs, and you can request a tailored quote for your specific move and storage needs.

When storage makes sense

Combining removals and storage with one company keeps things simple, and there are plenty of situations in Burgess Hill where storage proves invaluable. The most common is a gap in the chain: you complete on your sale before your purchase is ready, and rather than camp on a relative's floor surrounded by boxes, your belongings go safely into storage until the keys are in your hand.

Storage is also useful when you are renovating your new home and want it clear of furniture while the work is done, when you are downsizing and not quite ready to part with certain pieces, or when you are moving abroad in stages. Some people simply need to declutter a home for sale, as a tidy, spacious property shows far better to buyers. Whatever the reason, having your removals and storage handled by one insured firm avoids the hassle of coordinating separate companies. If you are weighing up the benefits of short-term storage in particular, our piece on the reasons to use short-term storage goes into more detail.

Matching the service to your move

Burgess Hill moves come in all sizes, so it pays to match the service to the job. For a full family home, a complete house removals service with an experienced crew takes the strain off entirely. For a flat, a few items or a single delivery, our man and van service from £80 is the economical choice. If you are moving a business rather than a home, our commercial removals service is geared to minimising downtime so you are back up and running quickly.

Packing is the part most people underestimate. Our full packing service handles the entire job, while fragile packing protects glassware, mirrors, ceramics and artwork. If you own antiques or a piano, our specialised antiques moving and piano moving services handle these properly, drawing on our LAPADA expertise.

Questions to ask a Burgess Hill moving and storage firm

A short conversation before you book reveals a great deal. Ask how long the firm has operated and whether the crew on the day are employed and trained by the company rather than brought in casually, because continuity shows in the care taken with your belongings. Ask specifically how their storage works: whether it is clean, dry and secure, how access is arranged if you need to retrieve something mid-storage, and how billing works for longer stays.

It is worth asking what happens if completion is delayed on the day, as it sometimes is, and whether they can hold your goods on the van or move them straight into storage at short notice. This flexibility is one of the real advantages of using a single firm for both removals and storage, since there is no second company to coordinate with when plans shift. Finally, confirm exactly what the quote includes, from packing materials to any storage handling fees, so the final invoice holds no surprises. A reputable firm welcomes these questions and answers them plainly.

Timing your move in and around Burgess Hill

When you move matters as much in Burgess Hill as anywhere. Fridays and the final working days of each month are the busiest, as that is when most completions land, so those slots get booked early. If you have any flexibility, a mid-week, mid-month move tends to be easier to schedule and more relaxed on the day. Summer and the run-up to Christmas are peak seasons, while the quieter winter months often offer more choice of dates.

Because Burgess Hill is such a popular family town, with demand shaped by school catchments and the commuter calendar, the weeks before a new school term can be especially busy. Whatever your timing, confirming your date with your removal and storage company as early as possible gives you the best chance of securing the slot and the crew you want. If storage is bridging a gap in your chain, booking it alongside your removal from the outset keeps everything joined up and avoids last-minute scrambling.

Planning a smooth move in Burgess Hill

A little preparation makes any move run far more smoothly. The earlier you start, the calmer the whole process feels.

Declutter first

The cheapest thing you can do for any move is reduce how much you are moving. Work through each room honestly and pass anything you no longer use to charity shops, the recycling centre or online marketplaces. If you are downsizing, our advice on downsizing your home helps you make those decisions without losing what matters. Decluttering also keeps storage costs down, since you only pay to store what you genuinely want to keep.

Sort access and parking

Check the access at both properties and arrange parking for the removal lorry where needed, particularly on the older streets near the town centre or where a new-build estate keeps the lorry some distance from the door. Mention any access concerns when you book so the crew can plan around them.

Pack an essentials box and read meters

Keep a labelled box of things you will want immediately, such as the kettle, mugs, tea, phone chargers, toiletries and medication, and keep it with you rather than in the van. Take meter readings at both properties on the day, and locate the stopcock and fuse board in your new home. Our helpful moving tips cover the full run-up, and our guide on planning for a house move sets out a sensible timeline.

Moving to and from Burgess Hill

Many of the moves we handle are not contained to the town itself. People relocate from Burgess Hill out to the coast, across to neighbouring towns like Haywards Heath and Hassocks, into Horsham, or much further afield. Because we cover West and East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, as well as offering nationwide and European removals, we can manage a local move within Burgess Hill or a relocation to the other end of the country with the same care and the same insured storage available throughout.

Making the most of combined removals and storage

One of the quiet advantages of choosing a firm that handles both your removal and your storage is how much smoother the logistics become. When the same team that loads your home also runs the storage facility, there is no double handling between separate companies, no second set of paperwork, and no risk of one firm blaming another if something goes awry. Your belongings are inventoried once, handled by people you have already met, and kept under a single insurance policy from your front door to wherever they finally end up.

It also makes the eventual move out of storage far simpler. When the keys to your new home are ready, the same firm that knows your inventory can deliver and place everything, often without you having to lift a finger beyond pointing to which room each box belongs in. For anyone juggling a chain, a renovation or a relocation in stages, that continuity removes a surprising amount of stress. It is one of the reasons we encourage Burgess Hill customers who anticipate any gap in their move to talk to us about removals and storage together, right from the first conversation, rather than treating them as two separate problems to solve.

Bringing it all together

Choosing a moving and storage company in Burgess Hill comes down to a few sensible checks: proper insurance that covers both transit and storage, genuine independent reviews, secure and flexible storage, a clear itemised quote, and real local knowledge of the town's streets and homes. Handling removals and storage through one trusted firm keeps the whole process simple. As a Checkatrade-verified, LAPADA-member family business, fully insured with cover up to £10 million and serving mid Sussex since 2016, we would be glad to take the strain off your move. Call us on 01903 893731 or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk whenever you are ready to talk it through.
faqs: Do you offer both removals and storage in Burgess Hill?

Yes. We provide a full removals service and secure, flexible storage, and handling both through one insured firm keeps your move simple. Whether you need storage for a few days while the chain catches up or several months during a renovation, your belongings stay protected throughout.
How much does a move in Burgess Hill cost?

It depends on the size of your home, the access at both properties, the distance and how much packing or storage you need. Our man and van service starts from £80, while a full house move is priced according to the work involved. We always provide a clear, itemised quote up front.
Is my furniture insured while it's in storage?

Yes. We are fully insured with cover up to £10 million, which protects your belongings through loading, transit and storage. It is always worth asking any firm how their storage is secured and insured before you commit, and we are happy to explain exactly how we keep your items safe.
When should I consider using storage during a move?

Storage is invaluable when there is a gap in the chain between selling and buying, when you are renovating your new home, when downsizing and not ready to part with certain items, or when moving abroad in stages. It also helps declutter a property for sale so it shows better to buyers.
Do you only cover Burgess Hill?

No. We are based near Pulborough and cover West and East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, with nationwide and European removals available too. We handle local moves within Burgess Hill, moves to neighbouring towns like Haywards Heath and Hassocks, and long-distance relocations alike.
